title: IPO six-month price-action predictor
question: Can prior IPO research be turned into a reusable six-month price-action framework using offering mechanics, underwriters, holder control, market setup, index flow and business quality?
finding: Yes, as a transparent scorecard rather than a price oracle. The model starts with the negative IPO-chase prior from studies 15 and 24, then adjusts for offering quality, underwriters, holder/control risk, market setup, index flow, lock-up overhang and business quality. SpaceX screens as a negative six-month bucket with a -20.2% midpoint from first listed close, 22.4% probability of beating SPY and 18.4% probability of beating QQQ: underwriters, scarcity and Nasdaq-100 fast-entry flow help, but valuation, unlock supply and Musk/Class B control dominate the six-month setup.
